Studio Business Plan â€” Hartford Dance Fitness Studio (working title)
Vision: a welcoming movement studio in Frog Hollow or Parkville for all levels.
Target opening: 18-month horizon; lease decision gated on $18k startup reserve.
Startup cost estimate: $18,000 first year (buildout, mirrors, sound, insurance).
Dream fund: Marcus Savings at Nutmeg FCU â€” kept separate, currently building.
Space option A: Parkville storefront, ~1,400 sq ft, ~$2,100/mo.
Space option B: Frog Hollow ground floor, smaller, closer to home.
Revenue: class packs, drop-ins, private lessons, showcase ticketing.
Pricing draft: 8-class pack $120, drop-in $18, private $60/hr.
Instructors: start with me + Rosa; add one assistant when volume supports it.
Tech stack: WordPress + WooCommerce storefront, Stripe payments, Typeform intake.
Marketing: Instagram @michellekemp.dance, Mailchimp newsletter, showcase promos.
Branding: Figma logo drafts from Tommy; pick direction by end of quarter.
P&L projection: build monthly model, break-even target month 9.
Risk: overlap with agency schedule; protect Tue/Thu class blocks always.
Milestone: tour three spaces this fall and shortlist by winter.
Milestone: finish the choreography workbook manuscript for a launch product.
Comps: pulled Parkville and Frog Hollow lease comps from Zillow.
Next action: confirm QuickBooks vs Xero for the studio books before launch.
Note: no lease signed. This is a disciplined plan, not a leap.
Class schedule model: Tue/Thu evening, Sat morning, winter intensives seasonal.
Community angle: partner with the Coalition for a low-cost neighborhood class.
Retention idea: makeup-class credits and a class-pass loyalty tier.
Showcase cadence: fall and winter, ticketed via Eventbrite/Stripe.
Reserve rule: do not touch the Marcus fund for anything but the studio.
